question_title: Will the interest in “the players championship” change between 2026-03-17 and 2026-03-27 according to Google Trends?
summary: The Players Championship 2026 ended Sunday March 15 (peak interest = 100). March 17 is just 2 days post-peak, still in the rapid decay phase (historical peak+2 values: 4 in 2024, 7 in 2025; estimated ~3-5 for 2026). March 27 is 12 days post-peak, when interest will have fully returned to baseline (0-2). The outcome depends critically on whether March 17 rounds to 3 (→ Doesn't Change, since diff would be only -2 to -3) or 4+ (→ Decreases, since diff would exceed -3). The 2024 analogy (most similar peak+1 magnitude) favors March 17 = 4-5, slightly favoring Decreases. The 2025 decay ratio applied to 2026 gives ~3, favoring Doesn't Change. This is a genuine knife-edge scenario. P(Increases) is negligible since there's no mechanism for renewed interest in a completed tournament.

factors.description: Tournament ended Sunday March 15 (peak=100). March 17 is peak+2 (Tuesday). Historical peak+2 values: 2024=4, 2025=7. With March 16=19 (peak+1), March 17 likely 3-5.
March 27 is 12 days post-tournament. Historical data shows values drop to 0-2 within 4-5 days of peak. March 27 virtually certain to be 0-2.

Critical boundary: if March 17 = 3, the difference with March 27 (0-1) is only -2 to -3, resolving as Doesn't Change. The 2025 decay ratio (7/50=0.14) applied to 2026 gives 19*0.14=2.7, rounding to 3.
The 2024 analogy (peak+1=17, most similar to 2026's 19) gives peak+2=4, which with March 27 at 0-1 would resolve as Decreases.
SerpAPI may differ from pytrends by ±2-3 points. At low values (3-5), this measurement noise could flip the outcome across the ±3 threshold.
No upcoming golf events or Players Championship news expected by March 27. P(Increases) is near-zero.

reasoning:
**Forecast: Decreases (55%) vs Doesn't Change (44%) vs Increases (1%)**

This is a knife-edge question. The Players Championship peaked on Sunday March 15 (value=100) and is in rapid post-tournament decay. The critical question is what Google Trends reports for March 17 (2 days post-peak):

- **If March 17 ≥ 4** (and March 27 ≈ 0-1): difference exceeds ±3 → **Decreases**
- **If March 17 ≤ 3** (and March 27 ≈ 0-1): difference within ±3 → **Doesn't Change**

The 2024 historical analogy (most similar in peak+1 magnitude: 17 vs 2026's 19) suggests peak+2 = 4-5, slightly favoring Decreases. The 2025 decay ratio suggests ~3, favoring Doesn't Change. With SerpAPI measurement uncertainty adding further noise, this is genuinely close to a coin flip, with a slight edge to Decreases.
resolution_criteria: This question resolves “Doesn't change” if the value on the timeline at [https://trends.google.com/trends/explore?geo=US&tz=0&q=the%20players%20championship&date=2026-02-25%202026-03-27](https://trends.google.com/trends/explore?geo=US&tz=0&q=the%20players%20championship&date=2026-02-25%202026-03-27) for 2026-03-27 is within 3 of the value at 2026-03-17. It resolves “Increases” if the value is more than 3 greater, and resolves “Decreases” if the value is more than 3 lower.
fine_print:
A script will be used to determine the resolution of this question. It will access the data on Google Trends using [SerpApi](https://serpapi.com/), and compare the height of the timeline for the two aforementioned dates. The specific python query will be structured as follows:
```
from serpapi import GoogleSearch

search = GoogleSearch(params={
  "api_key": API_KEY,
  "engine": "google_trends",
  "data_type": "TIMESERIES",
  "geo": "US",
  "tz": 0,
  "q": "the players championship",
  "date": "2026-02-25 2026-03-27",
})
search.get_dict()
```
Note that there may be differences between the results returned by the API and the data appearing on the page. This seems to be due to the 'tz' parameter not having the intended effect in-browser. In this case, the API results will be considered authoritative.
Note that the precision of the timeline will be 1 day, so this will compare the overall interest for the whole day as determined by Google Trends.
Dates are determined in UTC.
If the data is no longer available, or the script fails, this question will be annulled or manually resolved by a moderator.
